content.text: I found header parsing references in `src/reader.rs` and `src/parser/mod.rs`; next I’ll inspect the relevant code and existing inline tests to understand the expected behavior.

I found the header parser: it currently reads everything after `%PDF-` until end-of-line and requires it to be UTF-8, which rejects binary bytes on the header line. Next I’ll inspect the main load path and existing parser tests so I can add a focused temporary regression test.

diff --git a/src/parser/mod.rs b/src/parser/mod.rs
index 279d650..400d228 100644
--- a/src/parser/mod.rs
+++ b/src/parser/mod.rs
@@ -411,10 +411,12 @@ fn _indirect_object<'a>(
 
 pub fn header(input: ParserInput) -> Option<String> {
     strip_nom(map_res(
-        delimited(
+        preceded(
             tag(&b"%PDF-"[..]),
-            take_while(|c: u8| !b"\r\n".contains(&c)),
-            pair(eol, many0_count(comment)),
+            terminated(
+                take_while_m_n(3, 7, |c: u8| c.is_dec_digit() || c == b'.'),
+                pair(take_while(|c: u8| !b"\r\n".contains(&c)), pair(eol, many0_count(comment))),
+            ),
         ),
         |v: ParserInput| str::from_utf8(&v).map(Into::into),
     ).parse(input))

content.text:
Implemented the fix in `src/parser/mod.rs`.

What changed:
- Relaxed PDF header parsing so lopdf extracts only the version portion after `%PDF-`.
- The parser now accepts trailing bytes on the header line, including non-UTF-8/binary bytes like:
  ```text
  %PDF-1.3 \xb0\x9f...
  ```
- It still requires the `%PDF-` prefix and a newline-terminated header line.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test reproducing the binary-bytes-on-header-line failure.
- Confirmed it failed before the fix and passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary test as requested.
- Ran final sanity test:
  ```text
  cargo test --lib load_short_document
  ```
  Result: passed.

No commit was created.

result:
Implemented the fix in `src/parser/mod.rs`.

What changed:
- Relaxed PDF header parsing so lopdf extracts only the version portion after `%PDF-`.
- The parser now accepts trailing bytes on the header line, including non-UTF-8/binary bytes like:
  ```text
  %PDF-1.3 \xb0\x9f...
  ```
- It still requires the `%PDF-` prefix and a newline-terminated header line.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test reproducing the binary-bytes-on-header-line failure.
- Confirmed it failed before the fix and passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary test as requested.
- Ran final sanity test:
  ```text
  cargo test --lib load_short_document
  ```
  Result: passed.

No commit was created.
